source.description: Six-panel raster contact sheet generated for the May 25, 2026 old-fashioned bicycle with basket sketch lesson, reviewed before cropping.
source.notes: Prompt required one consistent side-view bicycle process with no embedded labels, arrows, numbers, signatures, watermarks, or fake UI.

process_strategy: The sequence builds the wheel circles, frame triangle, handlebar and seat, front basket, spokes, tire shadows, and restrained warm pencil accents before the final panel only darkens keeper lines and clarifies existing details.

final_elements.name: two bicycle wheels
final_elements.description: Two round wheels aligned in a simple side view.

final_elements.name: frame triangle
final_elements.description: A clean diamond/triangle frame connecting both wheels.

final_elements.name: seat and handlebar
final_elements.description: A small seat and curved handlebar placed on the existing frame.

final_elements.name: front basket
final_elements.description: A small woven basket attached above the front wheel.

final_elements.name: spokes and chain
final_elements.description: Light wheel spokes plus a simple chain line already attached to the frame.

final_elements.name: restrained basket tint and tire shadows
final_elements.description: Soft warm color on the basket and light graphite shadows under the tires.

frames.visible_job: Blocks in two aligned wheel circles with light construction marks.
Adds the frame triangle connecting the two wheels.
Places the small seat and curved handlebar on the existing frame.
Adds the front basket above the front wheel without changing the bicycle pose.
Adds light spokes, a simple chain, restrained basket tint, and tire shadows to existing forms.
introduces: restrained basket tint and tire shadows

final_step.visible_job: Darkens keeper contours, clarifies the spokes, chain, handlebar, seat, and basket weave, and balances the existing warm tint and tire shadows.
allowed_changes: restrained pencil tint and shadow deepening

rejection_checks: No wheel, frame, seat, handlebar, basket, spoke, chain, tint, or tire shadow should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
